Farm Groups Warn U.S. Agriculture Nearing Brink - AGDAILY More than 50 agricultural groups are warning that mounting economic and global pressures are pushing U.S. farmers to the brink, calling on the Trump administration for additional relief. In a March 19 letter led by the American Farm Bureau Federation and signed by 54 agriculture organizations, industry leaders outlined a growing list of challenges — from extreme weather to rising input costs and geopolitical disruptions — that they say are threatening the viability of U.S. farms. “This combination of sustained market pressures, weather-related challenges, and geopolitical uncertainty poses a serious threat to the upcoming growing season and the long-term viability of U.S. agriculture,” the letter states. “Food security is national security, and America’s farmers call on you to support additional resources during this turbulent time to ensure a strong, reliable and affordable domestic food supply for communities across the country.”. Without timely assistance, continued losses risk accelerating farm closures, reducing domestic production capacity and weakening the ability of farmers and ranchers across this great nation to provide food, clothes and fuel for the American people.”. https://www.agdaily.com/news/farm-groups-warn-u-s-agriculture-nearing-brink/

DHS Shutdown Could Come Back to Bite Democrats - Newsweek Senate Democrats blocked Department of Homeland Security funding in mid-February, betting that public anger over immigration enforcement would sustain their leverage and extract meaningful reforms to ICE and Customs and Border Protection. Democrats had spent the Biden years defending themselves against attacks over border security and immigration enforcement. This time, Democrats thought, they could turn immigration into Republican vulnerability. Yet Republicans have blocked Democratic proposals to fund those agencies separately. Democrats have put forward bills six times to fund TSA, FEMA, CISA, and the Coast Guard without ICE and CBP, allowing negotiations to continue on immigration enforcement. Only one Democrat voted to advance full DHS funding: Senator John Fetterman, a Democrat from Pennsylvania. The Democratic position: we've already funded ICE and CBP substantially; we won't provide additional funding without immigration enforcement reforms.". While 55 percent disapprove of how ICE and CBP enforce laws in cities, voters oppose the shutdown because they want enforcement to proceed, not because they oppose it. https://www.newsweek.com/dhs-shutdown-could-come-back-to-bite-democrats-11693248

Border security chief steps down after 18 months in the job - BBC Martin Hewitt's appointment as the UK's Border Security Command had been presented as a "a significant step forward in the fight against illegal migration and criminal smuggling gangs". Conservative shadow home secretary Chris Philp said the government's "complete failure on small boats" was the fault of the prime minister and home secretary rather than Hewitt. "Over the past 18 months, the Border Security Command has brought government agencies, law enforcement and international partners together to tackle people smuggling gangs, as well as seeing the Border Security, Asylum and Immigration Act into law.". Despite the Labour government's promise to tackle the trafficking gangs behind the Channel crossings, the number of people entering the UK on small boats has continued apace while Hewitt was in the role. Responding to his departure, the Conservative shadow home secretary Chris Philp said: "The government's complete failure on small boats isn't down to Martin Hewitt. https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2dn1x9l3jo
